seo.title: 100 Astro resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Astro resources for engineers.

content.intro: Astro is designed for content-heavy applications where performance and type-safety are paramount. This resource guide focuses on the practical implementation of Astro's content collections, the efficient use of the island architecture for dynamic interactivity, and scaling strategies for programmatic SEO and edge deployment.

sections.heading: Content Collections and Type-Safe Schema Management

items.title: Zod Schema Validation
items.description: Define strict frontmatter schemas in src/content/config.ts using Zod to ensure all markdown files contain required metadata like publishDate and author.

items.title: MDX Component Mapping
items.description: Use the <Content components={{ h1: CustomHeading }}> syntax to override standard HTML elements with custom Astro or React components globally.

items.title: Discriminated Unions in Zod
items.description: Implement z.discriminatedUnion in collections to handle different types of content (e.g., 'video' vs 'article') within the same directory.

items.title: Automated Slug Generation
items.description: Override default slug generation by defining a custom 'slug' field in the Zod schema and using it in getStaticPaths for SEO-optimized URLs.

items.title: Reference API for Relations
items.description: Use the reference() function in Zod to create relationships between collections, such as linking an 'author' entry to a 'blog' entry.

items.title: Content Layer API (Experimental)
items.description: Utilize the experimental content layer to fetch data from remote APIs or CMSs while maintaining the local Content Collection developer experience.

items.title: Markdown Image Resolution
items.description: Configure the image() helper in Zod schemas to automatically process and optimize local images referenced in markdown frontmatter.

items.title: Type-Safe Routing with getEntry
items.description: Use the getEntry('collection', 'slug') function to fetch specific items with full TypeScript autocompletion for all frontmatter fields.

items.title: Custom Markdown Plugins
items.description: Inject remark-gfm or rehype-autolink-headings into astro.config.mjs to extend markdown functionality for technical documentation.

items.title: RSS Feed Generation
items.description: Implement @astrojs/rss using content collection data to automatically generate valid XML feeds for blog subscribers.

items.title: Client:visible Directive
items.description: Use client:visible to delay hydration of heavy components (like complex charts) until they enter the user's viewport.

items.title: Client:idle for Low Priority
items.description: Apply client:idle to components like newsletter signups that don't need immediate interactivity, reducing initial TBT.

items.title: Nano Stores for State Management
items.description: Use Nano Stores to share state between multiple frameworks (e.g., React and Svelte) on the same page without a heavy runtime.

items.title: Svelte for Lightweight Islands
items.description: Integrate @astrojs/svelte for UI components that require minimal JavaScript bundle size compared to React or Vue.

items.title: Pagefind for Static Search
items.description: Implement Pagefind as a post-build step to provide full-text search capabilities without a server-side backend or heavy JS library.

items.title: Astro Image Component
items.description: Replace standard tags with from astro:assets to automate WebP conversion and layout shift prevention.

items.title: View Transitions API
items.description: Enable the <ViewTransitions /> component in your layout to provide SPA-like navigation feel between static pages.

items.title: Persistent Islands
items.description: Use the transition:persist directive to keep a component state (like an audio player) alive across page navigations.

items.title: Prefetching Strategies
items.description: Configure the prefetch: true setting in astro.config.mjs to automatically load link data when users hover over internal links.

items.title: Client:only for Browser APIs
items.description: Use client:only="react" for components that strictly require browser globals like window or document to prevent SSR errors.

sections.heading: Scaling for pSEO and Edge Deployment

items.title: Dynamic getStaticPaths
items.description: Generate thousands of pages for programmatic SEO by mapping large JSON or CSV datasets to the getStaticPaths return array.

items.title: Cloudflare Pages Integration
items.description: Deploy Astro using the @astrojs/cloudflare adapter to leverage Edge Functions and fast static asset delivery.

items.title: Hybrid Rendering Mode
items.description: Set output: 'hybrid' in config to keep most pages static while enabling SSR for specific routes like auth or search results.

items.title: Edge Middleware for Geolocation
items.description: Use middleware.ts to detect user location and serve localized content or redirect to regional subdomains at the edge.

items.title: On-demand Incremental Static Regeneration
items.description: Configure Vercel or Netlify adapters to use On-demand Revalidation for updating specific pages without a full site rebuild.

items.title: External Data Fetching in Build
items.description: Fetch data from external APIs directly inside the frontmatter of .astro files to bake dynamic data into static HTML at build time.

items.title: Sitemap Module Configuration
items.description: Use @astrojs/sitemap to automatically generate a sitemap.xml that includes all dynamic routes generated by getStaticPaths.

items.title: Environment Variable Validation
items.description: Use the astro:env (experimental) or a custom Zod validation script to ensure required API keys are present during build.

items.title: Parallel Build Optimization
items.description: Utilize the build.concurrency setting in astro.config.mjs to speed up static generation for sites with 10,000+ pages.

items.title: Partytown for Third-Party Scripts
items.description: Offload analytics and tracking scripts to a web worker using @astrojs/partytown to keep the main thread clear for user interactions.

pro_tips: Use the 'astro check' command in your CI/CD pipeline to catch Zod validation errors and TypeScript issues before deployment.
Avoid using React components for simple layouts; use .astro files to ensure zero JavaScript is sent to the browser for structural elements.
Leverage the 'Astro.slots' API to create highly flexible, reusable layout components that can handle named content areas.
For pSEO sites, store your dataset in a SQLite database and query it during the build process to avoid memory overhead of large JSON files.
Use the 'public' folder only for assets that don't need processing; use 'src/assets' for everything else to benefit from Astro's image optimization pipeline.
